content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 26, 1992

Billie J. Fangman
Deputy City Clerk
City of Marysville
Post Office Box 150
Marysville, CA 95901

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our Advice No. I-92-264

Dear Ms. Fangman:

You have requested advice concerning the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ Your request is one for
general assistance. Accordingly, we are treating it as a request
for informal advice.2/

QUESTION

Are members of Marysville's newly created Board of Zoning
Adjustment (the "board"), who were previously the planning
commissioners for the City of Marysville, required to continue to
file Commission Form 721, Statement of Economic Interests?

CONCLUSION

The members of Marysville's Board of Zoning Adjustment are
considered "planning commissioners" under the Act and must
continue to file Commission Form 721, Statement of Economic
Interests, with the Fair Political Practices Commission (the
"Commission").

_________________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-92-264
Page 2

FACTS

On May 7, 1991, Marysville's City Council repealed Chapter
2.40 of the Marysville Municipal Code and abolished Marysville's
Planning Commission. The city council then enacted a new Chapter
2.40 establishing the board of zoning adjustment for the purpose
of dividing the duties of the planning commission between the city
council and the board.

The board is comprised of five members, each appointed by a
city councilmember, subject to the concurrence of majority vote
by the city council. The functions of the board are to administer
the zoning code of the city and to review use permits and variance
applications. Although the board does not review zoning or
general plans, it does have the authority to make informal
recommendations to the city council regarding planning matters.

ANALYSIS

Members of all planning commissions organized under
Government Code Section 65100 are "planning commissioners" within
the meaning of Section 87200. (West Advice Letter, No. A-82-162.)
Government Code Section 87200 enumerates the type of public
officials required to file Commission Form 721, Statement of
Economic Interests. Planning commissioners are among those
listed. All positions listed in Section 87200 are required to
file their Statements of Economic Interests, with their agency's
filing officer who in turn makes and retains copies and forwards
the originals to the Commission. Accordingly, Marysville's Board
of Zoning Adjustment members must continue to file utilizing
Commission Form 721, Statement of Economic Interests with
Marysville's city clerk.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.3

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[signature]
By: Cheryl A. Hoff
Staff Services Analyst
Legal Division

SH:CAH:ch

3 Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
